mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero (below 0.25%) signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases duration-sensitive asset valuations. This mechanism operates continuously when the policy rate is in the lower bound regime, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of 2021-02-15 indicates zero lower bound regime
regime_hint: monetary accommodation at zero lower bound
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.25% are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in the zero-bound regime.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 200,000 persons signals labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market participants revise growth forecasts upward.
failure_condition: Positive payroll surprises (> 200k monthly change) do not produce positive 5-day forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) above 3% year-over-year combined with housing starts (HOUST) above 1,200 thousand units signals broad-based demand expansion that supports equity valuations. This interaction mechanism requires both demand channels to be simultaneously strong, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth > 3% AND HOUST > 1200 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 447,715 million and HOUST at 1,206 thousand as of 2021-01-31
failure_condition: When both RSAFS YoY > 3% and HOUST > 1200k are triggered simultaneously, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness or demand destruction that reduces corporate profit margins and future earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines do not produce negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year while federal funds rate remains below 0.50% creates a negative real rate environment that initially supports equities but signals future monetary tightening risk. This regime-conditional mechanism produces positive returns in early-cycle inflation but flips negative as tightening becomes imminent, with a 20-day forward horizon capturing the transition.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 238.099 as of 2021-01-31 and FEDFUNDS at 0.14% as of 2021-02-15
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 0.50%, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism reverses to negative returns in > 40% of instances after 2021-Q3.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward and recession risk increases.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession risk
failure_condition: UNRATE > 5.5% does not produce negative 60-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 3% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth and multiple expansion. This always-on trend mechanism produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when growth momentum is above trend.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16324.3 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2020-10-31
failure_condition: Periods of GDPC1 growth > 3% annualized do not produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 65% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE < 5.0%) signals tight labor markets and supply constraints that compress profit margins through wage inflation, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as the market reprices earnings quality downward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.0793 and UNRATE at 5.1% as of 2021-02-28
regime_hint: tight labor market with supply constraints
failure_condition: When INDPRO monthly growth > 0.5% and UNRATE < 5.0% are simultaneously triggered, 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
